disputedBy.claim: Allied intelligence prior to D-Day placed only 716. ID on the Omaha bluffs; the forward deployment of 352. ID — including 916 IR holding the cliff line — was a significant intelligence failure documented by Zetterling against pre-invasion Allied estimates.

waypoints.note: Defensive deployment: 916 IR forward on the Omaha bluffs (Vierville–Colleville–Sainte-Honorine); 914 IR west toward Pointe-du-Hoc / Grandcamp; 915 IR (Kampfgruppe Meyer) inland reserve. HQ at Littry / Saint-Lô. Position is a rough division centroid behind the bluffs.
H-Hour: 916 IR engages first US assault waves from the bluff line; defensive positions intact along most of Omaha frontage.

disputedBy.claim: Sources disagree on the exact timing and direction of Kampfgruppe Meyer's commitment. Meyer was first ordered east against airborne reports near the Orne, then redirected to Bayeux/Omaha — arriving too late to influence the bluff line. Zetterling and Beevor differ on the sequence of orders.

waypoints.note: Kampfgruppe Meyer (915 IR + supports) ordered east toward British airborne reports, then redirected toward Omaha/Bayeux.
Bluff line breached at multiple points; 916 IR forward positions overrun or pinned. Division begins falling back toward Formigny / Trévières.
End of MVP window: defensive line collapsing on the Omaha frontage; no successful counterattack delivered. Centroid approximate behind the receding line.
